name: restore accepted base, then narrow locality window

summary.name: restore accepted base, then narrow locality window
summary.hypothesis: Restore the accepted grouped_rows=8 + reversed row-pair + right-left PTX sweep + one-sync handoff base, then test whether reducing only kFixedHotBandPtxGroupedRows from 8 to 4 improves locality on the accepted consumer path without reopening the rejected grouped_rows=16 path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band consumer-path locality and reuse window width on the accepted PTX sweep / handoff path.
summary.risk: Medium: shrinking the group size may be too small and lose the locality benefit that the accepted base already captures, but the change stays narrowly scoped.
metrics_to_recheck: cp.async wait / CTA handoff stall behavior
shared-memory replay or load conflict signals
summary.stop_condition: Only accept if it beats 25.634208 ms and keeps the accepted sweep order and handoff behavior intact.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
